# Nuthatch

> A self-hosted-first, AI-native blockchain indexer in one Rust binary. From a contract
> address to a live indexed API, with no mandatory third-party data service and nothing
> phoning home.

Nuthatch indexes EVM chains on your own hardware. Embedded mode runs as a single static
binary with no external services: no Postgres, no Docker, no IPFS. It is free and licensed
AGPL-3.0. Status: v0.5.0 (early 0.x software). This file separates what the binary does today from
the design targets, so a model does not present unbuilt features as present-tense fact.

Tagline: "Be your own indexer."

## What it does today

- `nuthatch init 0xAddr [0xAddr…] [--chain <mainnet|arbitrum-one|base>]` resolves each contract's
  ABI (Sourcify, then an Etherscan-class API; EIP-1967/legacy-OZ proxies resolved) and scaffolds a
  multi-contract nest: `nuthatch.toml`, `abis/`, a `schema.json`, a `semantic.toml` (per-nest
  meaning, seeded from the ABI), a commented `views/` starter, an `llms.txt`, and a Claude Code
  skill. Omit `--chain` and nuthatch probes each supported chain for the contract's bytecode. It
  does not yet auto-generate the view logic, handlers, or tests from the ABI.
- `nuthatch sql "…"` runs one-shot SQL from the terminal (aligned table, `--json` to pipe), or opens
  an interactive REPL (`.tables`/`.schema`, history) when given no query. `nuthatch add 0xAddr` grows
  a nest with another contract without re-init. `nuthatch mcp --print-config` prints a copy-paste MCP
  client config; systemd/docker recipes run `dev` unattended.
- Governed semantic layer (RFC-0016): the `semantic.toml` drives an enriched `schema` (meaning +
  hot/cold coverage seam + derived footguns); a failed query returns a one-line fix hint and `explain`
  validates a query without executing it; MCP results are compact tables with a citable provenance
  stamp (`as of block N, sealed_through M, registry <hash>`).
- `nuthatch dev` polls logs over RPC (round-robin failover, adaptive `getLogs` chunking), decodes
  every declared event of every contract (topic0 registry → one table per `{alias}__{event}`),
  stores rows in an embedded hot store (redb), seals finalized ranges to content-addressed Parquet
  (hot store pruned), and serves a local HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:8288: `/tables`, `/table/{name}`
  (hot+cold merged), `/schema` (the enriched data model), `/sql` (read-only DuckDB, sealed history
  UNION the live hot tip), `/explain` (validate SQL without running it), `/entity/{id}`, `/balances`,
  `/balance/{addr}`, `/exposure/{addr}`, `/flags`, `/ready` (readiness: 200 fresh / 503 stalled),
  `/metrics` (Prometheus), and a localhost-gated admin UI at `/_admin/`. Every row carries provenance columns (`block_hash`, `_seq`,
  `block_timestamp`) and `*_dec`/`*_overflow` siblings for big integers.
- A nest is a git repo: `nuthatch init --from <git-url | ./dir>` runs a published nest (vendored
  ABIs + deployment blocks, schema-version guarded); authored `views/*.sql` (RFC-0018) are a
  validated, drift-gated layer - each binds against the schema at load, so a moved column is a loud
  `nuthatch check` failure, described in `semantic.toml` and surfaced in `/schema`; `nuthatch check`
  also runs `checks/*.sql` against recorded fixtures (hermetic parity/invariant framework).
- Balances are an incrementally-maintained view (DBSP): i128 base units serialised as decimal
  strings, a reorg is a retraction, and the view replays from stored facts on restart.
- `dev --seal-direct` runs a ~20× faster phased cold-start backfill (measured ~289 → ~5,837 ev/s on
  public RPC, byte-identical segments). Operator surface: `/metrics`, `/ready`, `/sql` guards
  (timeout, row cap, concurrency), health-aware RPC failover (a failing endpoint is benched on a
  cooldown), startup segment recovery (hash-verify + quarantine), graceful shutdown on SIGTERM.
- `nuthatch transform <component.wasm>` runs a pure, zero-capability WASM transform over an Arrow
  batch. `nuthatch mcp` serves the Model Context Protocol over stdio (12 tools: status, schema,
  tables, table, sql, explain, entity, balance, top_balances, flags, exposure, screen_status; plus 3
  resources — nuthatch://schema|tables|status — and 3 prompts: profile-contract, investigate-address,
  verify-a-number), bridging offline to a running instance.
- Factories and dynamic contract discovery (RFC-0009): `[[templates]]`/`[[factories]]` index the
  child contracts a factory deploys at runtime (it indexes Uniswap), tip + backfill + reorg-safe,
  with a `{template}__children` view.
- Compliance pack (RFC-0008): sanctions screening against content-addressed list snapshots
  (`nuthatch screen`, replayable `sanction_hit` annotations), threshold/velocity flags at `/flags`,
  counterparty exposure at `/exposure/{addr}`, alert webhooks via a durable at-least-once outbox, and
  a signed pack manifest (`pack build`/`verify`, ed25519) with `audit replay`/`report`.
- Admin UI and webhooks (RFC-0010): an embedded read-only admin UI at `/_admin/` (no framework/CDN,
  localhost-gated), plus user `[[webhooks]]` that POST sealed rows matching a `where` clause,
  HMAC-signed with `X-Nuthatch-Signature`.
- Nest packaging and the roost (RFC-0012, complete): `nuthatch nest pack`/`mount` build and verify a
  content-addressed nest blob, reproducing the decode registry from the installed inputs; `nuthatch
  roost` runs several nests on one chain in a single process under one shared cursor, hot store, and
  finality view, with strict per-nest isolation and a per-runtime RSS budget. Verified live on Arbitrum.
- Chains: Ethereum mainnet, Arbitrum One (42161), and Base (8453), with finality as a per-chain
  policy. Measured footprint: ~37 MB single contract, ~58 MB three-contract 23-table nest (2.8% of
  the 2 GB budget; CI fails above 256 MB).

## Design targets (not built yet)

- reth ExEx tip-ingestion against your own node (designed and stubbed; toolchain + dependency
  blockers cleared, needs a synced node; RPC is the working path), and firehose-class extraction of
  traces and state diffs on top of it (RFC-0014, deferred).
- Scaled mode (Postgres hot store + DataFusion federation, RFC-0013 direction record; the
  SQL-over-the-tip half of RFC-0013 already ships - `/sql` unions hot and cold).
- Generated schema, views, and tests from an ABI or a subgraph manifest; a GraphQL compatibility
  layer; streaming subscribe (SSE); local AI models (Ollama / bring-your-own-key); effectful HTTP
  egress for transform worlds (`kv` grant ships today); wildcard indexing.

## How it works

- Ingestion (RPC extraction today; reth ExEx when colocated, designed) -> deterministic decode
  -> a hot store (redb) for point-reads at the tip -> content-addressed Parquet sealed past
  finality -> embedded DuckDB attached read-only for analytical SQL. Balances are declarative
  incremental views (IVM).
- Reorgs touch only the mutable hot store and become retractions in the incremental view. The
  columnar layer is append-only by construction.
- The escape hatch is capability-isolated WASM components via WIT. A component granted zero
  capabilities is a pure function and can be re-executed and verified; its purity is checkable
  from its imports alone.

## Principles

- Single static binary; zero external services in embedded mode.
- No phone-home: no telemetry, no mandatory API tokens, no gated data service, ever.
- Deterministic core: decode, reorg handling, and entity derivation are reproducible. LLMs
  generate code and tests; LLM output never sits in the runtime data path.
- AI-native today: a built-in MCP server, a `schema` tool, and a shipped `llms.txt` and Claude
  Code skill, all offline against your local instance. Local models (Ollama / BYO-key) are planned.

## Install

v0.5.0 is the current release. The installer and `cargo` fetch the latest.

- Prebuilt binaries for macOS (Apple Silicon) and Linux x86_64 on the GitHub release, with
  SHA-256 checksums; the one-line installer (`curl -fsSL https://nuthatch-indexer.com/install.sh | sh`)
  downloads and verifies them.
- `cargo install --git https://github.com/nuthatch-indexer/nuthatch nuthatch` (rustc 1.95 or newer).
- A Homebrew tap, an OCI image, and detached release signatures are still on the way.

## Worked example

`/example` rebuilds a real community Graph Protocol subgraph (PaulieB14/horizon-indexer-subgraph,
credited to Paul Barba) as a Nuthatch nest - Graph Horizon staking on Arbitrum One across three
contracts, one `init` over several addresses, per-event tables queryable during backfill,
AssemblyScript handlers rebuilt as declarative `views/*.sql` (reorgs retract; no rollback code),
and parity via `nuthatch check`. Most of it runs on v0.5.0; the auto-generated GraphQL layer, SSE
streams, and AI-assisted schema/test generation are marked as design targets.

## Who runs it

`/stories` answers "is this for me?" through three illustrative personas (not testimonials),
each mapped to one of the three front doors: an SQL endpoint for application backends, published
nests for analysts who write no indexing code, and the built-in MCP server for agents. These
workflows run on v0.5.0 - multi-contract nests, every declared event as a table, Arbitrum One and
Base, user-authored views, `init --from`, `/sql`, and MCP all ship, as do factories, the compliance
pack, the admin UI, and webhooks. The main forward-looking piece for these stories is a GraphQL
layer for subgraph-shaped clients. It notes that derived views read finalized data while raw event
tables are fresh to the tip.

## Roadmap

`/roadmap` is generated from the source repo, not from marketing copy: the README's working-now
table and progress log are the source of truth for what is shipped, and the numbered RFCs under
`docs/rfcs/` for what is planned. Shipped: the embedded pipeline end to end, nests, three chains
(Ethereum mainnet + Arbitrum One + Base), the measured ~20× seal-direct/pipelined backfill
(RFC-0004), the operator surface (`/metrics`, `/sql` guards, graceful shutdown), factory/dynamic
discovery (RFC-0009, it indexes Uniswap), the compliance pack (RFC-0008: screening, flags, exposure,
signed audit), a local admin UI + webhooks (RFC-0010), content-addressed nest packaging and the
multi-nest roost (RFC-0012), the delightful core (RFC-0015: sql/REPL, magical init, add, prod
recipes), and the governed semantic layer + agent-grade MCP (RFC-0016/0017). v0.5.0 is the current
release, with prebuilt binaries. In progress: the
Lodestar migration (RFC-0011, pilot live in production - two panels; horizon-nest carries the surface),
and reth ExEx wiring (RFC-0003, needs a synced node). Planned: the remaining release engineering
(RFC-0005: signing, Homebrew, OCI), scaled mode (RFC-0013: Postgres + DataFusion), firehose-class
extraction (RFC-0014), a GraphQL layer, IVM generalisation, and wildcard indexing. Not planned: a token or decentralized-network
features, telemetry, a Kubernetes-first deployment, and any gateway/auth/metering layer in the
core. It promises order, not dates, and defers to the README progress log when the two disagree.
